mode.description: Mode defaults to strict; progressive mode records recoverable apply errors and continues with later stages.

protectedInterfaces.description: ProtectedInterfaces excludes interfaces from routerd-generated adoption and management-path disruption checks.

context.description:
Context is the least-privilege allowlist of config resources the plugin
may read on stdin. Empty/absent = the plugin receives no configuration
(default-deny). Secrets are ALWAYS redacted from whatever is passed.

spec.description: DynamicOverridePolicySpec is defined in api because DynamicOverridePolicy is authored in startup config.

allowOverlap.description: AllowOverlap permits an address prefix that overlaps another configured IPv4 prefix.

exclusive.description: Exclusive removes other IPv4 addresses from the target interface before adding this address.

dnsSource.description: DNSSource defaults to self; dhcpv4 reuses DNS servers learned on dnsInterface, static uses dnsServers, and none omits DNS options.

routerSource.description: RouterSource defaults to interfaceAddress; static uses router, and none omits DHCP option 3.

state.description: State gates a resource on status fields from other resources.

profile.description: Profile applies provider-specific defaults; NTT profiles default prefixLength to 60 and DUID type to link-layer.

state.description: State gates a resource on status fields from other resources.

addressSuffix.description: AddressSuffix is ORed into the selected /64 to derive the final IPv6 address.

field.description: Field defaults to phase unless phase is set.

phase.description: Phase is shorthand for requiring the dependency phase field to equal this value.

prefixDelegation.description: PrefixDelegation references the DHCPv6PrefixDelegation resource that supplies the delegated prefix.

subnetID.description: SubnetID selects the /64 inside the delegated prefix; it defaults to 0.

delegatedAddress.description: DelegatedAddress references an IPv6DelegatedAddress used to derive self DNS and RA-adjacent settings.

resource.description: Resource names the source resource as Kind/name and reads its status, or a router config field for supported resources.

mode.description: Mode defaults to stateless; ra-only emits router advertisements without DHCPv6 service.

selfAddressPolicy.description: SelfAddressPolicy references a SelfAddressPolicy resource used when dnsSource is self.

addressSuffix.description: AddressSuffix defaults to the referenced delegated address suffix for delegatedAddress candidates.

ordinal.description: Ordinal is one-based when selecting an address from an interface.

source.description: Source selects how the candidate address is found: delegatedAddress derives from a delegated prefix, interfaceAddress scans live interface addresses, and static uses address.

aftrAddressSelection.description: AFTRAddressSelection controls how multiple AAAA records are selected; ordinalModulo wraps the ordinal by the answer count.

phase.description: Phase is shorthand for requiring the dependency phase field to equal this value.

enabled.description: Enabled defaults to true; set enabled: false to keep the tunnel disabled.

field.description: Field defaults to phase when omitted.

resource.description: Resource names the source resource as Kind/name and reads its status, or a router config field for supported resources.

localAddressSource.description: LocalAddressSource defaults to interface; delegatedAddress derives the tunnel source from localDelegatedAddress.

state.description: State gates a resource on status fields from other resources.

spec.description: ProviderActionPolicySpec gates whether routerd may execute plugin-proposed provider actions (ADR 0007, Phase 5).

allowUndo.description: AllowUndo permits best-effort rollback. The zero value (false) disables it.

allowedActions.description:
AllowedActions restricts which canonical action verbs may be executed.
Empty = none.

allowedCIDRs.description:
AllowedCIDRs restricts execution to actions whose target.address falls
within one of these CIDRs. Empty = no CIDR restriction.

allowedProviderRefs.description:
AllowedProviderRefs optionally restricts to specific CloudProviderProfile
references. Empty = no provider-ref restriction.

allowedProviders.description: AllowedProviders restricts which providers may be executed. Empty = none.

dryRunOnly.description:
DryRunOnly, when nil or true, permits only dry-run; live mutation is
rejected. Set explicitly to false to allow live execution (still gated by
Enabled, approval, and the allowlists).

enabled.description:
Enabled must be true before any execution is permitted. The bool zero
value (false) keeps execution disabled by default.

executionWindow.description:
ExecutionWindow optionally restricts execution to a time window. It is
validated leniently (free-form, cron-ish); empty = no window restriction.

maxActionsPerRun.description:
MaxActionsPerRun caps how many actions one execution run may perform. The
zero value means 0 (no actions): the operator must set a positive bound.

requireApproval.description:
RequireApproval, when nil or true, requires an operator approval before an
action is executed. Set to false only to allow policy auto-approve.

spec.description: EventGroupSpec declares a CloudEdge Event Federation bus identity (ADR 0006).

auth.description:
Auth is reserved for Phase 2 peer delivery (HMAC over the overlay). It is
accepted but unused in Phase 1.

mode.description: Mode selects the integrity scheme; only "hmac" (or empty) is recognized.

secretFile.description: SecretFile is a filesystem path to the shared HMAC key.

secretRef.description: SecretRef references a secret resource carrying the shared HMAC key.

listen.description:
Listen is the receiver bind for inbound peer pushes. Empty Address means
this node is push-only (no receiver).

nodeName.description:
NodeName is this node's identity within the group; it is stamped as the
sourceNode on events emitted into this group.

replayWindow.description:
ReplayWindow is a Go duration bounding the accepted message timestamp skew
for replay protection; empty is treated as "5m" downstream.

retention.description:
Retention bounds how many federation events and for how long the local
store keeps them. Empty/zero values mean unlimited.

maxAge.description:
MaxAge is a Go duration (e.g. "30m", "24h") bounding event age; "" means
unlimited.

maxEvents.description:
MaxEvents caps the number of retained events for the group; 0 means
unlimited.

state.description: State gates a resource on status fields from other resources.

spec.description: EventPeerSpec declares a remote node a routerd node pushes federation events to within an EventGroup (ADR 0006, Phase 2).

direction.description:
Direction selects delivery direction; only "push" is supported in Phase 2.
Empty defaults to "push".

endpoint.description:
Endpoint is the base URL to push to, e.g. http://10.99.0.7:8787. Required
for push delivery.

groupRef.description: GroupRef is the EventGroup this peer belongs to (required).

nodeName.description: NodeName is the remote peer node identity (required).

subjectPrefixes.description:
SubjectPrefixes optionally filters delivery to subjects carrying one of
these prefixes; empty delivers all.

types.description: Types optionally filters delivery to these event types; empty delivers all.

spec.description: EventSubscriptionSpec declares that received federation events matching a predicate trigger a local Plugin (ADR 0006, Phase 3): the cloud-side rule that turns an observed fact into a DynamicConfigPart via a trusted local plugin.

groupRef.description: GroupRef is the EventGroup whose events this subscription watches.

match.description:
Match selects which events fire the subscription. Types is required so a
subscription cannot blanket-trigger a plugin on every event in the group.

payload.description: Payload requires each listed key to equal the given value in the event payload.

sourceNodes.description: SourceNodes restricts to events whose sourceNode is one of these (loop/scope guard).

subjectPrefixes.description: SubjectPrefixes restricts to subjects with one of these prefixes.

types.description:
Types restricts to these event types (required, at least one), e.g.
routerd.client.ipv4.observed.

trigger.description: Trigger names the plugin to invoke and optional batching.

batchWindow.description:
BatchWindow optionally coalesces matched events into one invocation
(Go duration). MVP invokes per poll tick; this is accepted forward-compat.

debounce.description:
Debounce optionally delays invocation after the last matched event
(Go duration). Accepted forward-compat; MVP uses poll-tick batching.

pluginRef.description: PluginRef names the Plugin resource invoked for matched events.

spec.description: MobilityPoolSpec declares a selective-address mobility pool for the CloudEdge Mobility Control Plane.

authority.description:
Authority declares who arbitrates ownership. The MVP supports static
arbitration only. Empty means every node deterministically projects the
shared event stream locally.

mode.description:
Mode selects the arbitration scheme. Only "static" is supported in the
MVP; empty defaults to it.

nodeRef.description:
NodeRef optionally names the arbitrating node. When set, it must be one of
the member NodeRefs.

capturePolicy.description:
CapturePolicy declares how non-owner sites capture an address that has
moved.

mode.description:
Mode selects the capture behavior. Only "all-non-owner-sites" is supported
in the MVP; empty defaults to it.

deliveryPolicy.description:
DeliveryPolicy selects the pool-level delivery control plane. Empty means
bgp; owned /32s are advertised and remote /32s are learned through BGP.

mode.description: Mode selects delivery. Empty means bgp delivery.

groupRef.description:
GroupRef is the EventGroup whose observed/expired events can feed BGP
mobility ownership for this pool (required).

ipOwnershipPolicy.description:
IPOwnershipPolicy retains high-level failover intent for BGP-mode
mobility. Ownership itself is represented by BGP best-path.

members.description:
Members maps routerd nodes to the site they serve within the pool
(required, at least one).

items.description: MobilityPoolMember binds a routerd node to a site within a MobilityPool.

capture.description:
Capture declares how this member captures addresses currently owned by
another site.

target.description:
Target carries non-secret provider target hints such as region,
compartmentId, resourceGroup, nicName, or ipConfigName. It is copied to
provider ActionPlan.target; credentials and tokens do not belong here.

targetFrom.description:
TargetFrom resolves provider target hints from spec.values. Keys are
provider target keys and values are spec.values keys. Explicit target
entries take precedence over targetFrom.

delivery.description:
Delivery is retained for hand-authored SAM compatibility. BGP-mode
MobilityPool delivery does not require per-member delivery routes.

deliveryTo.description:
DeliveryTo optionally selects delivery per owner identity. It is retained
for SAM compatibility; BGP-mode mobility does not lower per-lease routes.

maintenance.description:
Maintenance carries declarative operator maintenance intent for this member.
Drained placement members are excluded from active capture selection.

nodeRef.description: NodeRef is the routerd node identity (matches federation sourceNode).

ownershipDiscovery.description:
OwnershipDiscovery optionally lets a cloud member discover locally owned
provider private IPs and emit them as observed federation facts.

leaseTTL.description:
LeaseTTL controls the emitted observed event expiry. Empty defaults to the
controller default.

mode.description: Mode selects the discovery backend. Empty/disabled does nothing.

pluginRef.description:
PluginRef optionally pins the inventory plugin. Empty resolves by
provider name or sole observe.providerPrivateIPs plugin.

providerRef.description: ProviderRef defaults to the member capture.providerRef.

scanInterval.description: ScanInterval bounds provider inventory calls. Empty defaults to 60s.

scope.description:
Scope narrows which provider private IPs become mobility ownership facts.
Empty keeps the historical broad scan behavior.

excludeAddresses.description:
ExcludeAddresses optionally denylists discovered addresses by IPv4 CIDR or
bare IPv4 address.

includeAddresses.description:
IncludeAddresses optionally allowlists discovered addresses by IPv4 CIDR or
bare IPv4 address. Empty means all pool addresses are candidates.

includePrimary.description:
IncludePrimary controls whether provider-primary private IPs may become
mobility-owned addresses. Nil defaults to true for backward compatibility.

selector.description: Selector optionally filters discovered IP records by provider tags/labels.

subnetRef.description:
SubnetRef optionally constrains the provider scan. Empty asks the plugin
to infer it from the self NIC.

subnetRefFrom.description:
SubnetRefFrom resolves SubnetRef from spec.values when SubnetRef is not
set explicitly.

placement.description:
Placement optionally places this member in an active/standby capture group.
When set, only the highest-priority non-drained member in the same group
captures provider-side addresses. Provider-secondary members that share a
site/providerRef with an existing placement group must declare placement.

priority.description:
Priority orders active/standby candidates. Empty/0 is auto-numbered
deterministically within the group as 10, 20, ... while preserving any
explicitly configured priorities.

profileRef.description:
ProfileRef selects a profile from spec.profiles.cloudCaptures. It is a
local shorthand for the node's capture/discovery details; remote members
should normally remain identity-only.

role.description:
Role selects the SAM side semantics for this node. It is separate from Site
so sites can be named for topology while role remains provider-agnostic.

site.description: Site is the location label the node serves within the pool.

staticOwnedAddresses.description:
StaticOwnedAddresses declares IPv4 /32 addresses in the pool that this
member owns without relying on observed-client federation events. It is
intended for on-prem static-IP segments.

mode.description:
Mode selects the mobility scheme. Only "selective-address" is supported in
the MVP; empty defaults to it.

prefix.description: Prefix is the CIDR managed by this pool, e.g. 10.88.60.0/24 (required).

profiles.description:
Profiles defines reusable capture/discovery fragments. Members opt in
with profileRef and can override any concrete field locally.

capture.description: Capture defaults for provider-secondary-ip cloud capture.

target.description:
Target carries non-secret provider target hints such as region,
compartmentId, resourceGroup, nicName, or ipConfigName. It is copied to
provider ActionPlan.target; credentials and tokens do not belong here.

targetFrom.description:
TargetFrom resolves provider target hints from spec.values. Keys are
provider target keys and values are spec.values keys. Explicit target
entries take precedence over targetFrom.

ownershipDiscovery.description: OwnershipDiscovery defaults for provider private-IP observation.

leaseTTL.description:
LeaseTTL controls the emitted observed event expiry. Empty defaults to the
controller default.

mode.description: Mode selects the discovery backend. Empty/disabled does nothing.

pluginRef.description:
PluginRef optionally pins the inventory plugin. Empty resolves by
provider name or sole observe.providerPrivateIPs plugin.

providerRef.description: ProviderRef defaults to the member capture.providerRef.

scanInterval.description: ScanInterval bounds provider inventory calls. Empty defaults to 60s.

scope.description:
Scope narrows which provider private IPs become mobility ownership facts.
Empty keeps the historical broad scan behavior.

excludeAddresses.description:
ExcludeAddresses optionally denylists discovered addresses by IPv4 CIDR or
bare IPv4 address.

includeAddresses.description:
IncludeAddresses optionally allowlists discovered addresses by IPv4 CIDR or
bare IPv4 address. Empty means all pool addresses are candidates.

includePrimary.description:
IncludePrimary controls whether provider-primary private IPs may become
mobility-owned addresses. Nil defaults to true for backward compatibility.

selector.description: Selector optionally filters discovered IP records by provider tags/labels.

subnetRef.description:
SubnetRef optionally constrains the provider scan. Empty asks the plugin
to infer it from the self NIC.

subnetRefFrom.description:
SubnetRefFrom resolves SubnetRef from spec.values when SubnetRef is not
set explicitly.

cloudCaptures.description:
CloudCaptures contains reusable cloud provider-secondary-ip capture
defaults. It deliberately models only cloud capture/discovery because
on-prem proxy-ARP authority must remain explicit and fail-closed.

staticHandovers.description:
StaticHandovers declares planned static-owned address movement from an
on-prem member to another member. The controller releases the from member
first and only projects the to member after observing the release event.

values.description:
Values carries non-secret per-router constants referenced by profiles.
It is intended for provider identifiers such as NIC, subnet, region, or
resource group names; credentials and tokens do not belong here.

phase.description: Phase is shorthand for requiring the dependency phase field to equal this value.

field.description: Field defaults to phase when omitted.

preferredSource.description:
PreferredSource programs the Linux route preferred source (RTA_PREFSRC).
It is useful for host-originated traffic that must use a stable logical
source address while still following an explicit delivery route.

enabled.description: Enabled defaults to true; set enabled: false to keep the check disabled.

phase.description: Phase is shorthand for requiring the dependency phase field to equal this value.

enabled.description: Enabled defaults to true; set enabled: false to exclude the candidate from selection and rendering.

field.description: Field defaults to phase when omitted.

resource.description: Resource names the source resource as Kind/name and reads its status, or a router config field for supported resources.

gatewaySource.description: GatewaySource declares whether gateway is static, learned from DHCP status, or intentionally absent.

state.description: State gates a resource on status fields from other resources.

mode.description: Mode selects the route policy shape: priority chooses one candidate, mark installs marked tables, and hash spreads flows across targets.

state.description: State gates a resource on status fields from other resources.

egressPolicyRef.description: EgressPolicyRef uses the selected device from an EgressRoutePolicy when egressInterface is omitted.

snatAddressFrom.description: SNATAddressFrom reads the SNAT address from another resource status or supported router resource field.
